pattern: To drop just below the array with a single swap, find the rightmost position that can decrease and swap it with the largest smaller value to its right.
hints: A single swap can only lower the array if some element has a strictly smaller element somewhere to its right.
Scan from the right for the first index whose value exceeds the next one; that is where a decrease is possible while staying as large as possible.
At that index, pick the largest value to its right that is still strictly smaller (the rightmost such on ties to maximize the result) and swap them.

edge_inputs.description: swap near the end of an increasing prefix
duplicates of the candidate value, must pick the rightmost smaller
fully descending swaps the last two
tie among smaller values picks the leftmost copy to maximize result

stress.description: random arrays over a narrow range to force duplicates and tie handling
stress.note: narrow range maximizes the tricky duplicate-tie cases
